name: KenGen — Kenya Electricity Generating Company
description: Kenya's largest electricity generator, operating geothermal, hydro, wind, and thermal power plants with an installed capacity of over 1,900 MW. KenGen leads Africa in geothermal energy development, particularly at the Olkaria complex in the Rift Valley.

name: Kenya Airways (KQ)
description: Kenya's national carrier and the Pride of Africa, operating flights to over 50 destinations across Africa, Europe, Asia, and the Americas. The government holds 48.9% and KLM Royal Dutch Airlines holds 7.8% of shares.

name: Kenya Power & Lighting Company (KPLC)
description: Kenya's sole electricity distributor and retailer, serving over 9 million customers nationwide. KPLC manages the national grid and is responsible for last-mile connectivity under the Last Mile Connectivity Project.

name: Kenya Ports Authority (KPA)
description: Manages and operates the Port of Mombasa, East Africa's largest seaport, and the Port of Lamu under the LAPSSET Corridor Development Authority. KPA handles over 35 million tonnes of cargo annually.

name: Kenya Railways Corporation (KRC)
description: Operates the Standard Gauge Railway (SGR) and the Metre Gauge Railway (MGR). The SGR, built with Chinese financing, connects Mombasa to Nairobi and Naivasha. KRC carries a KES 737.5 billion SGR debt obligation.

name: Kenya Pipeline Company (KPC)
description: Operates Kenya's petroleum pipeline network spanning over 900 km from Mombasa to Eldoret, with branches to Kisumu and Nairobi. KPC is a candidate for partial privatization under the 2025 government divestiture programme.

name: Kenya Broadcasting Corporation (KBC)
description: Kenya's national public broadcaster operating radio and television services in Swahili, English, and over 20 vernacular languages. KBC provides public interest broadcasting including government communications and educational content.
